Off Calendar Sep-04-2019 Set For Trial On May-18-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Staying Discovery In Civil Proceeding Pending Disposition Of Criminal Proceeding
Off Calendar Jul-30-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. LAURA K. SWANSON, ESQ. of Busby Zappala & Sanchez LLP (Tel: 925-299-9600) appearing for plaintiff Nadia Chan;
Atty. THOMAS W. PURTELL, ESQ. of Law Offices of Thomas W. Purtell (Tel: 415-722-6291) appearing via CourtCall for defendant Hal Rowland.

HEARNG RE: DEFENDANT HAL ROWLAND'S Motion For An Order Staying Discovery In Civil Proceeding Pending Disposition Of Criminal Proceeding.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Hal Eugene Rowland's motion for an order staying discovery pending disposition of criminal proceeding is denied. On May 23, 2019, Defendant entered a no contest plea to two criminal charges (one felony and one misdemeanor) and the court made a finding of guilt. By the terms of the deferred entry of judgment (DEJ) to which Defendant agreed, on November 22, 2019, if Defendant complies with specified terms and conditions, judgment will be entered and sentence imposed on the basis of the misdemeanor plea; if he is in violation of those terms, judgment will be entered and sentence imposed on the felony. The terms and conditions require that Defendant not suffer any new arrests or violate the stay away order. Thus, although the criminal matter is not yet finally disposed of, Defendant has been convicted and found guilty, and has not shown any substantial risk that any incriminating testimony he may give in this civil case will be used against him in criminal court in violation of his Fifth Amendment rights. Upon weighing the competing interests of the parties, the court determines that Defendant has not shown good cause to stay discovery. (See Keating v. Office of Thrift Supervision (9th Cir. 1995) 45 F.3d 322, 324; Avant! Corp. v. Superior Court (2000) 79 Cal.App.4th 876, 886; Pacers, Inc. v. Superior Court (2005) 162 Cal.App.3d 686, 688-689.)

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185, E-MAIL: tonyvaughn@yahoo.com (302/EPS)
